text: The wide variation in chemical structures and diverse biological functions of natural antioxidants (AOXs) have made it very difficult to accurately separate, identify and quantify the individual antioxidants present in a mixed food or biological matrix. Because of their mutual synergies, the total antioxidant activity is always more useful and effective in confirming the positive health effects of mixed food components. Unfortunately, no single reliable assay for antioxidant determination has yet been agreed upon as a standard for food labelling. Various antioxidant assays are now categorized mainly into two types of assays based on the principle of electron transfer (ET)- and hydrogen atom transfer (HAT)-based assays. Because of great differences in redox potentials, pH and effect of different solvents in different assays, the validity and reliability of the results obtained from the different methods are therefore not comparable. In this perspective, this IUPAC project will facilitate the exploration of new information concerning the identification and assessment of activities and interactions of AOXs, the more scientific basis and evaluation for the limitations and potentials of individual antioxidant assays and for making the antioxidant activity data for food and food ingredients more comparable and reliable. Each member of this task group will briefly introduce their personal experiences concerning various antioxidant assays.
